calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2010 Continued To Feb-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs;
Off Calendar Dec-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs;
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Dec-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. Everett A. Hewlett, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Steven L. Hammond, Esq. appeared for plaintiff B2Mobile, LLC, a California limited liability company.
Shawn Scheuer and Sam Darawish, executive officers for plaintiff, were placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant My Town Meida, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, formerly known as Secondmeta, LLC a Texas limited libility company, Homecentrix, LLC. a Delaware limited liability company, or Christian Hunter, an individual.
Defaults were entered against defendants on September 13, 2010.
Doe defendants were dismissed on November 8, 2010.

Exhibit--1--B2Mobile Insertion Order Agreement dated 4/27/09.........................ID/EVID.
Exhibit--2--Paypal from Christian Hunter to B2Mobile dated 7/20/01...................ID/EVID.
Exhibit--3--15 pages of billing statements from B2Mobile.............................ID/EVID.
Exhibit--4--Four pages of invoices from B2Mobile, 1/10, 2/10 & 3/10..................ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff B2Mobile, LLC, a California limited liability company, and against the Defaulted Defendants My Town Meida, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, formerly known as Secondmeta, LLC a Texas limited libility company, Homecentrix, LLC. a Delaware limited liability company, and Christian Hunter, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $378,979.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $42,861.48.
Attorney Fees - Reserved.
Costs - $1,533.50, for a total judgment in the amount of $423,373.98.

Counsel stipulates to the return of the Exhibits.

Counsel for plaintiff to submit a proposed judgment to the Court by 5:00 p.m. today, November 9, 2010.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2010 Continued To Dec-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212
